2603.17230.title: KANtize: Exploring Low-bit Quantization of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Efficient Inference
2603.17230.paper_summary: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) have gained attention for their potential to outperform Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) in terms of parameter efficiency and interpretability. Unlike traditional MLPs, KANs use learnable non-linear activation functions, typically spline functions, expressed as linear combinations of basis splines (B-splines). B-spline coefficients serve as the model's learnable parameters. However, evaluating these spline functions increases computational complexity during inference. Conventional quantization reduces this complexity by lowering the numerical precision of parameters and activations. However, the impact of quantization on KANs, and especially its effectiveness in reducing computational complexity, is largely unexplored, particularly for quantization levels below 8 bits. The study investigates the impact of low-bit quantization on KANs and its impact on computational complexity and hardware efficiency. Results show that B-splines can be quantized to 2-3 bits with negligible loss in accuracy, significantly reducing computational complexity. Hence, we investigate the potential of using low-bit quantized precomputed tables as a replacement for the recursive B-spline algorithm. This approach aims to further reduce the computational complexity of KANs and enhance hardware efficiency while maintaining accuracy. For example, ResKAN18 achieves a 50x reduction in BitOps without loss of accuracy using low-bit-quantized B-spline tables. Additionally, precomputed 8-bit lookup tables improve GPU inference speedup by up to 2.9x, while on FPGA-based systolic-array accelerators, reducing B-spline table precision from 8 to 3 bits cuts resource usage by 36%, increases clock frequency by 50%, and enhances speedup by 1.24x. On a 28nm FD-SOI ASIC, reducing the B-spline bit-width from 16 to 3 bits achieves 72% area reduction and 50% higher maximum frequency.
2603.17230.paper_summary_zh: Kolmogorov-Arnold 網絡 (KANs) 因其在參數效率和可解釋性方面潛在超越多層感知器 (MLPs) 而受到關注。<lb/>與傳統的 MLPs 不同，KANs 使用可學習的非線性激活函數，通常是樣條函數，以基樣條 (B-splines) 的線性組合形式表達。<lb/>B-spline 係數作為模型的可學習參數。<lb/>然而，在推斷過程中評估這些樣條函數會增加計算複雜性。<lb/>傳統的量化通過降低參數和激活的數值精度來減少這種複雜性。<lb/>然而，量化對 KANs 的影響，尤其是在降低計算複雜性方面的有效性，仍然在很大程度上未被探索，特別是對於低於 8 位的量化水平。<lb/>本研究調查了低位量化對 KANs 的影響，以及其對計算複雜性和硬體效率的影響。<lb/>結果顯示，B-splines 可以量化到 2-3 位而幾乎不損失準確性，顯著降低計算複雜性。<lb/>因此，我們研究了使用低位量化的預計算表來替代遞歸 B-spline 算法的潛力。<lb/>這種方法旨在進一步降低 KANs 的計算複雜性並提高硬體效率，同時保持準確性。<lb/>例如，ResKAN18 在不損失準確性的情況下，使用低位量化的 B-spline 表實現了 50 倍的 BitOps 減少。<lb/>此外，預計算的 8 位查找表使 GPU 推斷速度提高了最多 2.9 倍，而在基於 FPGA 的脈衝陣列加速器上，將 B-spline 表的精度從 8 位降低到 3 位使資源使用減少了 36%，時鐘頻率提高了 50%，並且加速提高了 1.24 倍。<lb/>在 28nm FD-SOI ASIC 上，將 B-spline 位寬從 16 位降低到 3 位實現了 72% 的面積減少和 50% 的最高頻率提升。
2603.17230.author: Sohaib Errabii et.al.
2603.17230.authors: Sohaib Errabii, Olivier Sentieys, Marcello Traiola
